front: Painting: central ceiling panel The King Governs Alone and Crossing the Rhine in the Hall of Mirrors
Painting: also designed Salons of War and Peace, the Ambassador Staircase, and seven rooms named for Roman deities at Versailles
Painting: queens of Persia grovel before Alexander; Sisygambis mistakenly kneels to Hephaestion; hung in the Mars Room
back: The Tent of Darius / The Family of Darius Before Alexander (Charles Le Brun)

front: Painting: Alexander on golden chariot pulled by elephants entering a city (one of four massive paintings for Louis XIV)
back: Entry of Alexander into Babylon (Charles Le Brun)

front: Artist: 'First Painter to the King' under Louis XIV; originated the 'Louis XIV style'
Artist: founded the French Academy; led the Poussiniste faction (drawing over color) against the Rubenistes
Artist: headed the Gobelins tapestry manufactory; painted Louis XIV Visiting the Gobelins Factory
Artist: decorated Vaux-le-Vicomte for Nicolas Fouquet
Painting: servants hold blue parasols flanking a mounted patron
back: Chancellor Séguier on Horseback (Charles Le Brun)

front: Artist: physiognomy treatise (Les expressions des passions de l'ame) with faces distorted to resemble animals; inspired Watson and the Shark and Reynolds's portraits
Artist: distant relative of Elisabeth Vigee Le Brun, portraitist of Marie Antoinette
Artist: The Sacrifice of Polyxena was found in the Coco Chanel suite at the Paris Ritz in 2013

back: History of Alexander series (Charles Le Brun)
